title: ThreatEncounteredPayload
description: A hostile mob episode around one villager's body, narrated by the threat watcher (survival cluster). Edge-triggered on episode STATE, never per-pass: spotted once when the episode opens; engaged re-emitted only when the response flips (none->fight->flee, monotonic); killed/escaped close the episode; overwhelmed is rate-limited to once per 60s with the episode left open. Victim-only fanout. Producer: minecraft-service on world.events.

threatType.description: The nearest hostile's entity name (zombie, skeleton, creeper, ...). Free string — new mobs must not need a contract commit.

phase.description: spotted = episode opened (the mind's chance to preempt); engaged = a maneuver started or the response flipped; killed = the threat died and the episode closed; escaped = the body broke contact and the episode closed; overwhelmed = the maneuver is not working (cornered, out-damaged) — the one moment only the mind can change the plan.

response.description: The body's current maneuver; null before one is chosen (spotted) or when none applies.

count.description: Hostiles tracked in the episode at emission time.

distance.description: Blocks to the nearest tracked hostile at emission time.

detail.description: Human-readable color for prompts and the ledger: what attacked, how the fight ended, why the maneuver failed.
